name: Courtland Sutton
headline: Sutton's Touchdowns Hide Thinning Volume in Crowded Denver
body:
Sutton's WR12 finish in 2025 was built on opportunity more than dominance: a 21.2% target share, 124 targets across 17 games, and a 34.4% air-yards share that kept him the engine of Denver's downfield game. The efficiency, though, slipped from the prior year, with EPA per play climbing to 0.359 but yards per route run dipping to 1.74 and a 0.212 target-per-route rate that trails his 2024 mark. Seven scores on 74 grabs and 19 red-zone looks carried the fantasy line.

That touchdown dependence is the worry now. Bo Nix has leaned on him two straight seasons, but the arrival of Jaylen Waddle splinters a passing tree with little fresh volume to absorb, nudging Sutton toward a 1B role. His 1,017 yards and contested deep work keep a floor intact, yet the ceiling narrows. Treat him as a WR3 worth grabbing around the mid-rounds rather than reaching early.
